title: Plugin setup resolver falls back to process.cwd(), loading attacker-controlled setup-api.js (RCE)
description: resolveSetupApiPath resolves a bundled plugin's setup-api file by checking a list of repoRootCandidates, which included process.cwd() in addition to the canonical package/repository root. When a bundled extension's setup-api was not found at its real location, the resolver would look under <cwd>/extensions/<plugin>/setup-api.{js,ts,...} and return that path. The returned path is then loaded and executed by resolveSetupRegistration via getJiti(setupSource)(setupSource). Consequently, running an OpenClaw command (e.g. provider/model status resolution) from an attacker-controlled directory containing extensions/<plugin>/setup-api.js results in arbitrary JavaScript execution in the OpenClaw process under the current user (CWE-94).
